threats.scenario: JWT authentication token stored in localStorage (frontend/src/app/Services/request.interceptor.ts:13-16). Any XSS vulnerability on any page can steal the token via localStorage.getItem('token'), enabling full account takeover. Token has 6-hour lifetime.
threats.controls_in_place: None — token directly readable by JavaScript

remediation.title: Migrate JWT from localStorage to HttpOnly Secure cookie via BFF pattern
steps: Implement a BFF (Backend-for-Frontend) layer that holds tokens server-side
Issue HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite=Lax session cookie to browser
Remove all localStorage.setItem('token') calls from frontend

threats.scenario: bypassSecurityTrustHtml used on user-controlled data in multiple components: feedback comments (about.component.ts:119), lastLoginIp (last-login-ip.component.ts:39), user emails in admin view (administration.component.ts:60). This disables Angular's XSS protection, enabling stored XSS from user-supplied content.
threats.controls_in_place: sanitize-html 1.4.2 on server side (old version, bypasses known)

remediation.title: Remove bypassSecurityTrustHtml; use Angular's built-in interpolation or DOMPurify
steps: Replace bypassSecurityTrustHtml with Angular's {{ }} interpolation for text content
If HTML rendering is truly needed, use DOMPurify.sanitize() before passing to sanitizer.bypassSecurityTrustHtml

threats.scenario: Angular auth guards (app.guard.ts:18) check localStorage.getItem('token') presence, not validity. Client-side routing guards are purely advisory — they can be bypassed by direct URL navigation. Admin and accounting roles are determined by JWT payload which is client-readable and relies on server-side verification being consistent.
threats.controls_in_place: Server-side JWT verification on protected API routes

remediation.title: Ensure all sensitive data/actions have server-side authorization; add token validity check in guard
steps: Client guards are defense-in-depth only — ensure API enforces authorization
Add token expiry check in canActivate: jwtDecode(token).exp > Date.now()/1000
Never trust client-side role checks for actual data access

threats.scenario: User email stored in localStorage and sent as custom X-User-Email header (request.interceptor.ts:20-23). This header is used for user context in some routes but is not cryptographically verified — any script with localStorage access can read the email, and any client can forge the X-User-Email header.

remediation.title: Remove X-User-Email header; derive email from JWT token server-side
steps: Remove localStorage email storage and X-User-Email header
Server-side: extract email from verified JWT token payload
